Exiled leader to revive push for change amid US backing of Delcy Rodríguez and delays to democratic transition
Venezuela’s opposition leader, María Corina Machado, will seek to revive her push for political change with a rally in Madrid on Saturday, having found herself sidelined by Donald Trump after the abduction of the president Nicolás Maduro.
“Venezuela will be free,” the Nobel peace prize winner insisted in an interview on the eve of this weekend’s demonstration in the Puerta del Sol square, which is expected to draw tens of thousands of protesters.

articles.title: Odd Lots: Why Economists Might Be Getting AI Wrong (Podcast)
articles.description: Everyone knows that new technologies can be really disruptive to the labor market, but eventually new jobs emerge and things come back into balance. And there is a sense in which many view AI with the same lens. Yes, there will be pain in some sectors, but then there will be productivity gains and new sources of demand and new opportunities for labor that we can’t conceive of yet. But could it be different this time? Could AI be disruptive in a manner that, say, the steam engine was not? On this

topStories.description: Two congressmen resign in disgrace, the president gets in hot water, and Rory McIlroy joins an exclusive club.

topStories.title: Iran reimposes 'strict control' over Strait of Hormuz, citing continued U.S. naval blockade
topStories.description: Iranian military officials said Saturday that conditions in the Strait of Hormuz have reverted to "strict control," citing the continued U.S. blockade of its ports, just one day after declaring the waterway “completely open.”“Control over the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state, and this strategic waterway is now under strict management and control of the armed forces,” a spokesperson for Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said.“Until America allows

topStories.title: Strait of Hormuz shut by Iran again, Tehran accuses US of violating deal to reopen it
topStories.description: Iran warned that it would continue to block transit through the strait as long as the US blockade of Iranian ports remained in effect

topStories.title: Trump says U.S. has 'good news' on Iran, talks to continue over weekend
topStories.description: President Trump tells reporters that the U.S. and Iran will jointly recover nuclear material after a deal is reached.

topStories.title: China cautiously stakes out behind-the-scenes role to end Iran war
topStories.description: As Trump pursued an unpopular war, Beijing positioned itself as a peacemaker, with little incentive to pressure Iran or annoy the U.S.

dailyNews.title: Opposition defeated an attack on the Constitution, says Rahul Gandhi
dailyNews.description: Congress leader assures Prime Minister Narendra Modi of Opposition’s ‘unconditional support’ if the government wants to bring the 2023 women’s reservation law into force

dailyNews.title: Pakistan PM, army chief wrap up key trips in push for more US-Iran talks
dailyNews.description: Asim Munir leaves Tehran while PM Shehbaz Sharif heads home from Turkiye with hopes of another round of US-Iran talks.

dailyNews.title: Trump Sees Iran Deal As Imminent, Nuclear Issue Remains In Limbo
dailyNews.description: The president also floated the threat of resuming strikes on Iran once the current ceasefire expires next week.

dailyNews.title: Iran Reimposes 'Strict Control' On Hormuz After Trump Says US Naval Blockade Will Stay
dailyNews.description: Tehran has accused Washington of breaching an understanding on reopening the crucial shipping route, saying restrictions will stay in place until the US lifts its blockade of Iranian ports.

dailyNews.title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
dailyNews.description: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

dailyNews.title: We spoke to over 30 central bankers, policymakers and politicians. Here are their top concerns
dailyNews.description: From stagflation to energy security, policymakers spoke to CNBC about the biggest risks arising from the ongoing U.S.-Iran war.

dailyNews.title: Is Congress having a reckoning over sexual misconduct – or just a moment?
dailyNews.description: The resignations of two lawmakers facing ethics investigations have drawn comparisons to the #MeToo movement. Some House members say it’s time to revisit their rules and culture.

dailyNews.title: Not just Trump: Redistricting race will hinge on what these state leaders did
dailyNews.description: An 82-year-old Virginia senator raising the stakes, an Indiana consensus builder and a Texas enforcer are among state officials who have shaped the course of the midterm redistricting race.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  The internet’s favorite monkey continues capturing hearts
dailyNews.description: Punch the monkey’s fame is growing. Juju Chang traveled to the Ichikawa City Zoo in Japan to see for herself why the world went bananas for the tiny macaque and his heartwarming backstory.

dailyNews.title: Pope Leo heads to Angola in landmark Africa visit amid Trump clash
dailyNews.description: Leo is the third pontiff to visit the fossil fuel-rich country after John Paul II in 1992 and Benedict XVI in 2009.

dailyNews.title: 'Shouldn't be considered failure of govt ...' Rijiju on women's reservation bill not clearing LS
dailyNews.description: The government faced a setback as the women's reservation bill failed to pass in the Lok Sabha, requiring a two-thirds majority. Union Minister Kiren Rijiju blamed the Congress and opposition parties, calling it an anti-women stance and a significant blow to the nation. The opposition, while supporting reservation, opposed its linkage with delimitation and census.

dailyNews.title: Centre's conspiracy to change federal structure defeated; victory of Opposition unity, says Priyanka
dailyNews.description: Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra demanded that the government implement the Women's Reservation Act of 2023 on the current strength of the Lok Sabha.

dailyNews.title: Dems sidestep past ‘refuse illegal orders’ demands as they challenge Trump’s Iran war authority
dailyNews.description: Democrats backed U.S. troops in the Iran conflict but say President Trump's use of military force lacks congressional approval under the War Powers Act.

dailyNews.title: Iran could use detained Americans as ‘sweetener’ in nuclear talks, ex-hostage envoy warns
dailyNews.description: Roger Carstens, former lead hostage negotiator, warns Iran is untrustworthy as the Trump administration considers new round of peace talks in Pakistan.

dailyNews.title: Venezuela’s Machado to hold Madrid rally as opposition frozen out after Maduro capture
dailyNews.description:
Exiled leader to revive push for change amid US backing of Delcy Rodríguez and delays to democratic transition
Venezuela’s opposition leader, María Corina Machado, will seek to revive her push for political change with a rally in Madrid on Saturday, having found herself sidelined by Donald Trump after the abduction of the president Nicolás Maduro.
“Venezuela will be free,” the Nobel peace prize winner insisted in an interview on the eve of this weekend’s demonstration in the Puerta del Sol square, which is expected to draw tens of thousands of protesters.
 Continue reading...

dailyNews.title: Wesley sparks USWNT to 3-0 victory over Japan
dailyNews.description: Kennedy Wesley recorded her first international goal to lead the United States to a 3-0 friendly win over Japan on Friday night in Commerce City, Colorado.

dailyNews.title: Iran disputes claims of new agreements with Trump
dailyNews.description: Tehran put limits on the Strait of Hormuz’s opening, as the United States issued a new sanctions exemption on the sale of Russian oil.

dailyNews.title: 'Black day for Centre, they felt a shock for first time': Priyanka on delimitation bill defeat
dailyNews.description: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra hailed the defeat of the Delimitation Bill as a victory for democracy, accusing the Centre of a conspiracy to alter the federal structure. The bill, which proposed women's reservation linked to delimitation, failed to secure the required majority in the Lok Sabha. Opposition parties, while supporting women's reservation in principle, opposed its linkage with delimitation and census.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. will continue blockade after Iran says strait "completely open"
dailyNews.description: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.

dailyNews.title: Trump plans to ease access to psychedelics like psilocybin, ibogaine
dailyNews.description: The president will direct federal agencies to boost research into the drugs and support clinical trials, moves championed by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

dailyNews.title: Trump Will Participate in a Marathon Bible Reading
dailyNews.description: He will read a passage from the Old Testament that his Christian supporters cite as a call to national repentance and divine blessing.

dailyNews.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration made the announcement hours after Iran said that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial ships.

dailyNews.title: Trump Is Urged to Move on Nuclear Site Thought to Be Beyond Reach of Bombs
dailyNews.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.

dailyNews.title: Watch Pope Leo's visit to Cameroon, drawing over 100,000 worshippers
dailyNews.description: Pope Leo XIV visited Cameroon on his tour of Africa. He garnered a crowd of around 120,000 people for mass in Douala. He also spoke with students and faculty at the Catholic University of Central Africa and attended a Catholic hospital. The visit came amid his ongoing feud with President Trump.

dailyNews.title: Trump set to read Scripture from the Oval Office during 'America Reads the Bible' event starting Sunday
dailyNews.description: The president is set to read 2 Chronicles 7 during the 'America Reads the Bible' event, a passage organizers say was deliberately reserved for him.

dailyNews.title: U.S. delegation visited Cuba last week as Trump heaped pressure on island
dailyNews.description: A delegation of senior State Department representatives traveled to Cuba via a U.S. government plane last week, officials said, a diplomatic opening amid intense pressure from the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
dailyNews.description: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Iran pushes back on Trump's claims about agreement
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: RFK Jr clashes with Dem lawmaker over Trump’s mental fitness in heated exchange
dailyNews.description: RFK Jr. defended Trump's mental health during a tense congressional hearing, rejecting calls for a fitness assessment or invoking the 25th Amendment.

dailyNews.title: Trump says first releases from Pentagon UFO study will come out 'very, very soon' after Phoenix rally tease
dailyNews.description: President Trump says the first releases from a Pentagon study on UFOs will come out "very, very soon," teasing interesting documents found by officials.
